name: International Bank of Commerce
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal, business, and international banking services across Oklahoma and Texas with headquarters in Laredo.
description_long: International Bank of Commerce (IBC) is a publicly traded bank (NASDAQ: IBOC) headquartered in Laredo, Texas, serving customers across Oklahoma and Texas with comprehensive financial services. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing institutional stability and deposit protection. IBC offers a full range of personal banking products including multiple checking account options (Free Checking, Rite Checking, Interest Checking), savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and Visa debit/credit cards. For business customers, they provide commercial checking, business savings, small business loans, merchant services powered by Basys, treasury management solutions, and investment services including 401(k)s and pension plans. The bank also specializes in international banking with dedicated services for foreign nationals, international wire transfers, letters of credit, and currency exchange capabilities. IBC distinguishes itself through specialized focus on the Texas-Oklahoma market and explicit international banking expertise, particularly serving cross-border customers. They offer unique services like garage insurance, workers compensation, surety bonds, and remote deposit capture (IBC Deposit Express). The bank maintains a Spanish-language interface and demonstrates commitment to financial education through partnerships with Visa's Practical Money Skills program. As a regional bank, IBC provides personalized service and community presence that larger national banks may not offer. However, customers should note that branch availability is limited to Oklahoma and Texas, and the website content does not specify interest rates, fee structures, or loan terms—information essential for comparing competitiveness with larger national or online-only banks.
services: Free Checking, Rite Checking, Regular Checking, and Interest Checking accounts
Savings accounts and Certificates of Deposit (CDs)
Visa Debit Cards and Visa Credit Cards (personal and business)
Online Banking and Mobile Banking platforms
Small Business Loans and Commercial Checking
Merchant Services (powered by Basys) and payment processing
Treasury Management including Repurchase Agreement Sweep and Lockbox Service
International services: wire transfers, letters of credit, currency exchange, and foreign national loans
Business insurance (garage, workers compensation, property, auto, general liability, surety bonds)
Investment products: 401(k)s, pension plans, Simple IRAs, profit sharing, and investment banking (Gulfstar)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. government backing for account protection up to $250,000
Comprehensive international banking services including foreign national loan programs, currency exchange, and letters of credit
Multiple checking account tiers (Free, Rite, Regular, Interest Checking) providing options for different banking needs
Full-service business banking with treasury management, merchant services, and remote deposit capture (Deposit Express)
Integrated insurance products including garage, workers compensation, and business auto insurance
Online and mobile banking platforms plus overdraft courtesy options for personal banking
Spanish-language support (ESPAñOL) indicating accessibility for bilingual customers
cons: Geographic limitation to Oklahoma and Texas only—no national or online-only presence for out-of-state customers
Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APYs, or fee schedules for comparing account competitiveness
No mention of investment advisory services, wealth management, or high-net-worth banking despite having investment products
Limited information on loan products, terms, rates, or lending criteria for small business or personal loans
best_for: Small to mid-sized businesses in Texas and Oklahoma seeking integrated banking, insurance, and treasury management
Cross-border and international customers, including foreign nationals seeking U.S. banking access
Bilingual customers in Spanish-speaking communities in Texas and Oklahoma
diagnosis: IBC is best for customers in Texas or Oklahoma who need comprehensive banking plus integrated business insurance and international capabilities, particularly those requiring Spanish-language support or serving cross-border commerce. Main caveat: geographic limitation to two states and lack of transparent pricing information on its website make it unsuitable for out-of-state customers or those comparing rates across institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_mdi_status: Minority Board and Serving Hispanic Community
